role_summary: Mississippi rural civil rights movement participant; in this corpus's embedding her testimony clusters with Walter Bruce, Gertrude Newsome Jackson, Charles McLaurin, and Peggy Jean Connor.
ai_reading: Head's top semantic neighbors are Walter Bruce (entry 123, count 148), Gertrude Newsome Jackson (entry 136), Charles McLaurin (entry 17), Peggy Jean Connor (entry 96), and Nathaniel Hawthorne Jones (entry 92). The cluster is Mississippi-rural-movement throughout, the embedding reading her testimony as anchored in that region's specific organizing vocabulary. Her Tactical Pragmatism vs Strategic Vision position is +0.80, far toward Strategic Vision, and her Southern Struggle vs Northern Struggle position is +0.66, both readings reinforcing the regional anchor. The catalog's recurring pattern of Mississippi-rural-movement-women landing on the strategic side of the tactical/strategic axis (McLaurin's own +0.49, Henderson +0.51, McCullar +0.89, Lisa Anderson Todd +0.72) holds with Head's +0.80 too.
biographical_paragraph: Rosie Head's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 111 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. Independent biographical sourcing is pending; the strongest signal currently available for movement role is the embedding pattern visible in the AI's reading above.
